zh.Amaston, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾阿馬斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say於1184年出生在英國史洛普爾。他和Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY至少有1個女兒。他去世於1235年在英國史洛普爾阿馬斯頓享年51歲。

zh-hans.Amaston, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾阿馬斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say于1184年出生在英国史洛普爾。他和Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY至少有1个女儿。他去世于1235年在英国史洛普爾阿馬斯頓享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Ralf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: de'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say was born in 1184, in Shropshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. He died in 1235, in Amaston, Shropshire, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say est né en 1184, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. Il est décédé en 1235, à Amaston, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say wurde 1184 in Shropshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1235 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Amaston, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say è nato il nel 1184 nel Shropshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. Morì nel 1235, nel Amaston, Shropshire, England, all'età di 51 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say(은)는 1184년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1235년 영국 슈럽실르 아마스턴에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say nasceu em 1184, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. Ele faleceu em 1235, em Amaston, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say родился в 1184 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. Он умер в 1235 г. в местности амастон, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Ralf Roger de' Say nació en 1184, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mrs Roger Ralf de SAY. Murió en 1235, en Amaston, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años.
